healthy.name: Healthy
healthy.description: Leaves show uniform green colour, no spots or lesions, and intact margins. The plant is photosynthesising normally.
symptoms: Uniform green colour across the leaf
No spots, holes, or lesions
Firm, upright posture

early_blight.name: Early Blight
early_blight.description: A common fungal disease that attacks older, lower leaves first and forms characteristic concentric brown rings that resemble a target. Spreads in warm, wet weather and can defoliate plants if left untreated.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (bull's-eye pattern)
Yellow halo around each lesion
Older lower leaves affected first
Severe infection leads to defoliation

late_blight.name: Late Blight
late_blight.description: An aggressive water-mould disease responsible for the Irish potato famine. Spreads explosively in cool, humid weather and can destroy a crop within days. Acts on stems, leaves, and tubers.
symptoms: Large, irregular water-soaked lesions
White fuzzy growth on undersides of leaves in humid conditions
Rapid blackening and collapse of foliage
Brownish granular rot in tubers

healthy.name: Healthy
healthy.description: Leaves are deep green, free of lesions and pustules, and the plant is growing vigorously.
symptoms: Deep green leaf colour
No spots, streaks, or pustules
Upright growth and intact tassels

northern_leaf_blight.name: Northern Leaf Blight
northern_leaf_blight.description: A fungal disease that produces long, cigar-shaped grey-green lesions running parallel to the leaf veins. Favoured by moderate temperatures and prolonged leaf wetness. Yield losses can be heavy if infection starts before tasseling.
symptoms: Long elliptical (cigar-shaped) grey-green lesions
Lesions run parallel to the leaf veins
Lower leaves affected first, then spreading upward
Lesions turn tan and may merge

common_rust.name: Common Rust
common_rust.description: A fungal disease easily recognised by small reddish-brown powdery pustules scattered on both sides of the leaf. Cooler, humid conditions promote spread. Usually mild but can stunt growth if severe.
symptoms: Small reddish-brown powdery pustules
Pustules on both upper and lower leaf surfaces
Pustules later turn dark brown to black
Leaves may yellow and dry around lesions

gray_leaf_spot.name: Gray Leaf Spot
gray_leaf_spot.description: A fungal disease producing narrow rectangular lesions sharply bounded by leaf veins. Thrives in warm, humid conditions and continuous maize cropping. Can severely reduce kernel fill if it reaches the ear leaf early.
symptoms: Narrow rectangular grey-tan lesions
Lesions sharply bounded by leaf veins
Yellow halo when held against the light
Lesions merge causing whole-leaf blight

healthy.name: Healthy
healthy.description: Foliage is uniformly green, glossy, and free of lesions or insect damage. Stems are sturdy and the plant is fruiting normally.
symptoms: Uniform green leaves with no spots
Sturdy upright stems
Normal fruit set

bacterial_spot.name: Bacterial Spot
bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial disease producing many small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruit. Spreads quickly in warm wet weather and on contaminated tools or splashing water.
symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots that turn brown
Spots on fruit have a raised scab-like centre
Leaves may turn yellow and drop
Lesions can also appear on stems

early_blight.name: Early Blight
early_blight.description: A fungal disease producing concentric-ring lesions on older leaves. Lower foliage is hit first and can lead to extensive defoliation, exposing fruit to sunscald.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings
Yellow halo around lesions
Lower leaves affected first
Stem and fruit lesions in heavy infections

late_blight.name: Late Blight
late_blight.description: An aggressive water-mould disease that can destroy a tomato crop within days in cool damp weather. Affects leaves, stems, and fruit.
symptoms: Large pale-green to brown water-soaked patches
White mould growth on leaf undersides in humid weather
Rapid blackening and collapse of stems
Firm, greasy-looking brown patches on fruit

leaf_mold.name: Leaf Mold
leaf_mold.description: A fungal disease most common in humid greenhouses. Pale yellow spots appear on the upper leaf surface with a corresponding olive-green to brown velvety mould on the underside.
symptoms: Pale yellow blotches on upper leaf surface
Olive-green to brown velvety mould on leaf underside
Affected leaves curl, dry, and drop
Lower foliage affected first

septoria_leaf_spot.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
septoria_leaf_spot.description: A fungal disease producing many small circular spots with dark borders and tan-grey centres. Spreads upward from lower leaves and can defoliate plants if untreated.
symptoms: Many small circular spots (2–5 mm)
Tan-grey centres with darker borders
Tiny black dots (fungal fruiting bodies) inside spots
Heavy infection leads to leaf yellowing and drop

spider_mites.name: Two-Spotted Spider Mites
spider_mites.description: A pest infestation rather than a pathogen. Tiny mites feed on the underside of leaves, causing stippling and fine webbing. Populations explode in hot, dry conditions.
symptoms: Fine pale stippling (yellow speckles) on leaves
Bronze or yellow discolouration of foliage
Fine silken webbing on undersides and between leaves
Tiny moving specks visible with a hand lens

target_spot.name: Target Spot
target_spot.description: A fungal disease producing brown lesions with concentric rings resembling a target. Affects leaves, stems, and fruit, especially in humid conditions.
symptoms: Brown lesions with concentric rings
Lesions may have a yellow halo
Spots can coalesce and cause defoliation
Sunken brown lesions on fruit

yellow_leaf_curl_virus.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
yellow_leaf_curl_virus.description: A viral disease spread by whiteflies. Severely stunts plants and reduces fruit set; there is no chemical cure — control the whitefly vector and remove infected plants.
symptoms: Upward curling and crumpling of leaves
Pronounced yellowing along leaf margins
Severe stunting of new growth
Drastically reduced fruit set

mosaic_virus.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
mosaic_virus.description: A highly contagious viral disease producing mottled light-and-dark green patterns on leaves. Transmitted mechanically (contaminated hands, tools, tobacco). No cure — remove and destroy infected plants.
symptoms: Mottled light-and-dark green mosaic pattern
Curled, narrow, or fern-like leaves
Stunted growth and reduced yield
Uneven fruit colour or internal browning
